items.question: In agglomerative clustering, what is the starting configuration?
options: Each point as its own cluster
items.answer: Each point as its own cluster
items.explanation: Bottom-up agglomeration begins with n singleton clusters and merges pairwise until one remains.

items.question: Which linkage tends to produce chaining on elongated clusters?
items.explanation: Single linkage merges on the minimum cross-cluster distance, so one nearby bridge can connect distant blobs.

items.question: What does a horizontal cut through a dendrogram control?
options: The number of clusters at that resolution
items.answer: The number of clusters at that resolution
items.explanation: Cutting at height h partitions the tree into disjoint subtrees—each subtree is one cluster.

items.question: Ward linkage in scikit-learn requires which metric?
items.explanation: Ward's method is defined using squared Euclidean distances between cluster members.
